comment: 42yo female non-smoker, lifelong resident of high-PM2.5 industrial-adjacent urban district (annual PM2.5 average ~48 µg/m³, well above WHO 2021 Interim Target 1 of 35 µg/m³ and far above WHO Guideline 5 µg/m³). High-traffic-density residence on arterial roadway. No personal lung-cancer or pulmonary diagnosis. Outdoor air pollution IARC Group 1 (Monograph 109, 2013) — each 10 µg/m³ PM2.5 increment raises lung-cancer incidence ~9% (Hamra 2014; Pope 2020); KRAS/EGFR enrichment in non-smoker adenocarcinoma cohorts (Hill 2023). Engine fires RF-ENVIRONMENTAL-AIR-POLLUTION-LUNG-PREVENTION → routes to 2-track PreventionPlan: standard = exposure-mitigation counseling (indoor HEPA, N95 on high-AQI days, advocacy/relocation discussion), USPSTF lung screening eligibility review even at non-smoker baseline; aggressive = consideration of non-smoker LDCT enrollment in research protocols. Validates v0.3 environmental pilot. Anchored DIS-NSCLC.
